summary.title: Multi-Entity Corporate Accounting System - Phase 1 Django Models
summary.description: Foundational ORM models implementing CLAUDE.md invariants and docs/data-model.md schema

models.name: User
models.description: Application user extending Django's AbstractUser; UUID primary key for consistency

models.name: Entity
models.description: Legal entity in consolidated group; every financial record scoped to entity

models.name: EntityOwnership
models.description: Multi-tier ownership relationships with effective dates; no cycles constraint

models.name: UserEntityPermission
models.description: Per-user, per-entity role assignment; default-deny scoping; SoD override flag

models.name: Account
models.description: Per-entity Chart of Accounts; hierarchical; postability controls; currency restrictions

models.name: Period
models.description: Per-entity accounting period calendar; status=open/closed/locked; non-overlapping constraint

models.name: FXRate
models.description: Foreign exchange rates; spot/average/closing; ISO 4217 currency codes

models.name: JournalEntry
models.description: Accounting transaction header; immutable once posted; reversible via new entry; SoD controls

models.name: JournalLine
models.description: Individual debit/credit pair; exactly one of debit/credit non-zero; functional_amount signed

models.name: GeneralLedger
models.description: GL balance table for reporting; denormalized; populated by triggers; not posted to directly

models.name: AuditLog
models.description: Immutable append-only audit log; captures before/after state; INSERT only; no UPDATE/DELETE

constraints: indexes on jurisdiction, permissions, ownership relationships
unique(from_currency, to_currency, effective_date, rate_type) on FXRate

files.description: Pytest fixtures: user, entity, period, accounts (5 accounts), fx_rate, journal_entry_draft, journal_entry_posted

coverage: TestJournalEntryDoubleEntry: balanced entries, multi-line balancing, XOR debit/credit
TestJournalLineDecimalPrecision: Decimal type, high precision, currency codes
TestAccountTypes: all account types, normal balances, subtypes
TestAccountEntityScoping: entity FK requirement, code uniqueness per entity

sections: Core App Models (User, Entity, EntityOwnership, UserEntityPermission)
Finance App Models (Account, Period, FXRate, JournalEntry, JournalLine, GeneralLedger, AuditLog)

invariants_implemented: Immutability of posted entries (model + trigger enforcement)
Money as Decimal(20,4) with currency codes
Entity scoping on every financial record
Period locks preventing posting to closed/locked periods
FX rates with effective dates and rate types
design_decisions: UUID primary keys on all financial tables (consistency, security)
BigAutoField on AuditLog for efficient append-only inserts
Signed functional_amount on JournalLine for easy balance calculation
Transaction currency + functional currency tracking per line
Effective dates on EntityOwnership and UserEntityPermission
Status enum on JournalEntry for workflow state
Reversal via new entry (not status change) to maintain immutability
Dimension values as JSONB for flexibility
Trigger-based audit log for guaranteed capture

testing_stats.note: Property-based tests (Hypothesis) placeholders ready; fixtures designed for parameterized testing

db_level_constraints_to_implement: DEFERRED CHECK: sum(journal_lines.debit) = sum(journal_lines.credit) per transaction_currency
TRIGGER: Reject UPDATE/DELETE on posted journal_entries (except reversed_by_entry_id)
TRIGGER: Reject INSERT on journal_lines where parent entry status = 'posted'
TRIGGER: Reject INSERT/UPDATE on journal_entries where target period status != 'open'
TRIGGER: Audit log INSERT on every financial table mutation
EXCLUSION CONSTRAINT: periods non-overlapping per entity per type
before_production: Review all migrations for syntax and correctness
Create and test all db-level constraints (triggers, checks, exclusions)
Run full test suite: pytest apps/ -v --cov=apps --cov-report=html
Verify audit log trigger captures all mutations correctly
Load representative data (100+ entities, 10k+ journal entries) and test performance
Test immutability enforcement on posted entries
Verify entity scoping prevents cross-entity queries
Verify decimal precision in all financial calculations
Document any data migration needs for production cutover
Prepare rollback procedures if migration fails

notes.next_phase: Phase 2: Architecture (ADRs, threat model, deployment topology)
notes.db_triggers: Implemented via separate SQL DDL after migration application (not in Django migrations)
notes.type_hints: Full type hints in models; mypy strict mode ready for apps/finance/
notes.sod_enforcement: Model-level defaults; db-level enforcement via trigger on posting
notes.localization_ready: Entity.jurisdiction_country + local_attributes schema ready for per-jurisdiction modules
